raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California State Assembly voted to declare every August moving forward, Transgender History Month.",
    "Over 500 anti-LGBTQ bills have been introduced in State Legislatures across the country, with the majority targeting the human and civil rights of transgender people.",
    "Historians have confirmed that gender-nonconforming and trans people exist in historic written records dating back to antiquity.",
    "California has a rich and documented transgender history going back to the Spanish colonial era.",
    "The history of transgender people exists in ancient records from cultures such as Ancient Egypt, the Romans, China, and among Native Americans.",
    "San Francisco’s Tenderloin District has been home to transgender people since the 1800s.",
    "In 2017, San Francisco designated a small part of the Tenderloin as the Transgender Cultural District, making it the first legally designated area in the world of historic importance to the transgender community.",
    "The Compton's Cafeteria riots in August of 1966 are recognized as the first LGBT Civil rights uprising in the United States.",
    "In California, the first record of trans people dates back to 1775 when a Spanish soldier described native people who exhibited characteristics of both genders.",
    "The Gold Rush in California created an environment where transgender people could thrive.",
    "Charley Parkhurst, a transgender man, was a well-documented stagecoach driver in California.",
    "Lucy Hicks Anderson argued in court in the 1940s that she was entitled to her husband’s military pension as a woman.",
    "California was at the forefront of transgender legal rights."
  ]
}
```
